mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in nonfarm payrolls above 150k signals strong labor market demand, driving corporate earnings growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns in periods with PAYEMS > 150k are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.05 over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A concurrent rise in consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL > 3%) and housing starts (HOUST > 1.3M) signals strong demand pressures, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days due to inflationary risk premiums.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns in periods with CPIAUCSL > 3% and HOUST > 1300 are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC > 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A tightening cycle characterized by FEDFUNDS rising above 5% and CPIAUCSL exceeding 3% generates forward SPX returns declines due to heightened discount rate volatility and real rate uncertainty.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in periods with FEDFUNDS > 5% and CPIAUCSL > 3% are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production (INDPRO < 103.5) signals weakening supply chain capacity, creating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days through earnings downgrades.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in periods with INDPRO < 103.5 are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly contraction in real GDP (GDPC1 < 22.5T) indicates economic slowdown, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days through reduced corporate revenue expectations.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns in periods with GDPC1 < 22.5T are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate (UNRATE) below 4% signals tight labor market conditions, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days through wage inflation and corporate pricing power.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns in periods with UNRATE < 4% are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A concurrent rise in consumer credit (RSAFS > 700B) and housing starts (HOUST > 1.3M) signals strong private demand, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in periods with RSAFS > 700000 and HOUST > 1300 are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.05.
